description: Cloudflare Workers runtime gotchas and platform quirks

entries.bad_pattern: Calling new Date() or Date.now() at module top-level to compute values like CURRENT_YEAR
entries.good_pattern: Compute time-dependent values inside request handlers, not at module init
entries.description: Cloudflare Workers freeze the clock at the Unix epoch (Jan 1 1970) during module initialization — real time is only available once a request handler runs. Any `new Date()` or `Date.now()` call at the top level of a module (e.g. `const CURRENT_YEAR = new Date().getUTCFullYear()`) will return 1970, not the actual current year. This silently corrupts downstream logic like search queries that interpolate the year, LLM system prompts that reference 'the current year', rate-limit windows, cache keys, etc. Always move time-dependent computation inside a function that runs per-request. This is part of CF Workers' security model to prevent timing side channels before the first I/O.

entries.bad_pattern: ctx.waitUntil(longRunningJob()) for work that can exceed 30 seconds
entries.good_pattern: Either await the job inline in the fetch handler (15-min request-duration limit on paid plan) OR offload to Queues / Durable Objects / Workflows
entries.description: ctx.waitUntil() gets ONLY 30 seconds of extension after the fetch handler returns — regardless of `limits.cpu_ms` in wrangler.jsonc. `cpu_ms` bounds the fetch handler's active CPU time, it does NOT extend the waitUntil tail. If the tail exceeds 30s, CF kills it silently with a warn-level log (`waitUntil() tasks did not complete within the allowed time after invocation end and have been cancelled`) — no exception is raised in user code, so any `try/catch` inside the waitUntil job never runs. This makes background jobs look like hangs: D1 rows inserted as 'processing' stay 'processing' forever because the outer catch that would mark 'failed' is inside the killed isolate. The 15-min duration limit on paid plans only applies to the fetch handler's own lifetime (i.e. while it is still awaiting), not the tail. To run long jobs: (a) await them before returning the response (user waits for long POST), (b) use a streaming Response body so the handler itself stays active while the client reads, or (c) enqueue to CF Queues / Durable Objects / Workflows.
entries.context: Cloudflare Workers, long-running background work, src/pages/api.ts-style endpoints
